It's also HIPAA and SOC 2 compliant. satisfied 29 votes The SC R-2025 printable form is an essential document used for the renewal of the concealed weapon permit in South Carolina. This form ensures that applicants provide the necessary information to state authorities, enabling them to legally carry a concealed weapon for personal safety. Individuals seeking to renew their concealed weapon permit in South Carolina must complete the SC R-2025 form. This includes current permit holders whose permits are nearing expiration and those wishing to maintain their legal right to carry concealed weapons within the state. Filling out the SC R-078 form requires careful attention to detail to avoid common mistakes. Applicants should begin by clearly writing their personal information, including name, address, and date of birth. The form also requires proof of previous permit and any pertinent details that reflect the applicant's eligibility. To be eligible for renewal using the SC R-2025 printable form, applicants must meet certain criteria. They should be at least twenty-one years old, a resident of South Carolina, and demonstrate that they have not been convicted of a felony or any disqualifying misdemeanors. Additional criteria may include proof of safety training completed within the required timeframe. Carefully reviewing the completed SC R-078 form can prevent delays in processing. Common mistakes include providing incorrect personal details, failing to include necessary supporting documentation, and not signing the form where required. Ensuring all sections are completed legibly and accurately can significantly streamline the renewal process. Utilizing the SC R-078 printable form offers several benefits for permit holders. It simplifies the renewal process by providing a clear, structured format for applicants, ensuring that all necessary information is collected efficiently. Additionally, having a standardized form can expedite processing times with the state authorities. Once completed, the SC R-078 form can be submitted through designated channels set by the South Carolina Law Enforcement Division (SLED). Applicants should verify submission methods, ensuring that all documents reach the correct location. Submission may involve in-person delivery or mail, depending on individual circumstances. The SC R-078 form is used for renewing concealed weapon permits in South Carolina, ensuring compliance with state laws. In South Carolina, concealed weapon permits typically need to be renewed every five years. The SC R-078 printable form can be obtained through the South Carolina Law Enforcement Division's official website or offices. The most internationally recognised online TEFL courses lead to a level 5 qualification, which is the same level as the CELTA/Trinity CertTESOL. The minimum entry requirement for most well paid jobs, with the best packages, is a level 5 TEFL qualification. The online level 5 version of our TEFL course leads to a Level 5 TEFL Diploma (168 hours), it's regulated by Ofqual (UK government department) and awarded by Qualifi, a UK government recognised awarding body. If you are looking for an introduction to TEFL and plan to look for casual work teaching English, such as volunteering, teaching whilst backpacking and some online teaching roles, then we recommend you choose a level 3 online TEFL course. Our TEFL internships are open to anyone who has enrolled on our Level 3 or Level 5 courses and meets the requirements listed for each opportunity. An internship is the ideal way to kick-start your TEFL career whilst receiving a higher level of support than standard TEFL jobs. They are a good way to bridge the gap between qualifying and starting a more formal teaching role. Our voluntary TEFL positions are a great way to get started with TEFL, helping you to gain valuable experience whilst helping others and receiving additional support. They are open to anyone who has enrolled on our Level 3 or Level 5 courses and meets the requirements listed for each opportunity. How do online TEFL courses work? column13: finance frm frm part 1 frm part 2 garp quantitative finance risk management test prep Video Lessons 93+ hours Question Bank 4,000+ questions Students Enrolled 10,000+ Mastering Financial Risk Management (FRM) isn’t just about memorizing formulas—it’s about understanding how risk works in the real world. That’s why our FRM Part I & Part II Complete Online Course is designed to go beyond theory, using real-world examples, visual analogies, and exam-style practice questions to make complex concepts easier to grasp. Choose your package: Video Lessons for FRM Part I & Part II Online and Offline (Printable) Study Notes for FRM Part I & Part II Online and Offline (Printable) Question Bank for FRM Part I & Part II CBT Mock Exams for FRM Part I & Part II Formula Sheets Performance Tracking Tools Unlimited Ask-A-Tutor Questions Lifetime Access 24/7 Technical Support ● Learn Visually & Practically: Our FRM study notes and video lectures break down difficult topics using intuitive visual explanations, so you can see the logic behind risk models, valuation techniques, and financial markets. ● Real-World Applications: We bring risk management to life with case studies and industry scenarios, helping you connect concepts to real finance challenges. ● Exam-Style Questions & Mocks: Our FRM Part I & Part II practice questions mimic the difficulty level, structure, and style of the actual GARP FRM exams. ● Up-to-Date Study Materials: The question bank is regularly updated to reflect the latest GARP syllabus changes, so you’re always studying relevant content. Why settle for generic FRM prep course? Our course is designed to make learning engaging, practical, and exam-focused—giving you the best shot at success. Understanding the structure of the FRM Part I & Part II exams is essential for strategic preparation. The exam is rigorous and highly competitive, but with the right approach, you can maximize your chances of passing on your first attempt. FRM Part I focuses on the fundamental principles of risk management and the quantitative techniques used by financial professionals. It consists of 100 multiple-choice questions and is offered three times a year at computer-based testing centers. The exam is divided into four key topics, each contributing a specific percentage to your overall score: ✅ Foundations of Risk Management – 20% ✅ Quantitative Analysis – 20% ✅ Financial Markets and Products – 30% ✅ Valuation and Risk Models – 30% Pass Rate: Increase Your Chances of Passing – The FRM exam is known for its difficulty, but AnalystPrep’s proven study tools are designed to help you prepare smarter and score higher—just like thousands of successful candidates. Exam Strategy: Since Valuation and Risk Models and Financial Markets and Products account for 60% of the exam, focusing on these areas can significantly boost your score. FRM Part II takes a deeper dive into risk management applications, focusing on real-world financial risks across different sectors. It consists of 80 multiple-choice questions and is offered twice a year. The exam covers six key topics, each with a different weightage: ✅ Market Risk Measurement & Management – 20% ✅ Credit Risk Measurement & Management – 20% ✅ Operational & Integrated Risk Management – 20% ✅ Liquidity & Treasury Risk Management & Measurement – 15% ✅ Risk Management in Investment Management – 15% ✅ Current Issues in Financial Markets – 10% Pass Rate: The average pass rate for FRM Part II is around 50%—but keep in mind that by this stage, most candidates have already failed Part I at least once. Exam Strategy: Since Market Risk, Credit Risk, and Operational Risk together make up 60% of the exam, these should be your primary focus areas. We at AnalystPrep believe in transparency. By VIN By US License Plate YMM Search VIN View Sample Continue without VIN The Window Sticker, also known as Monroney labels or Monroney sticker, is a document attached to a new car by the manufacturer that details the vehicle’s standard features, optional equipment, engine and transmission specifications, warranty information, fuel economy ratings, and the Manufacturer’s Suggested Retail Price. Required by a 1958 bill sponsored by Oklahoma Senator Mike Monroney, this sticker provides essential, unbiased information for car buyers to compare vehicles and understand their features and costs. It is very important to get a window sticker. Without a window sticker, buyers don’t know the original specifications or price of the car. Sellers also look less trustworthy, and owners miss complete details about their vehicle. Getting a window sticker is important for both the seller, buyer, and car owners. Here are the reasons why: Helps You See the Vehicle’s Original Info: Getting a window sticker will help you see the car’s original specification, features, and MSRP. Helps to Negotiate Better: The window sticker has the complete information of the MSRP, and you can use the initial price to find the right price. Set the Right Price: Get the window sticker to help you set the price according to the MSRP and the specifications. Enhance Buyer’s Trust: Providing the window sticker helps you gain more trust from the buyers. Check Warranty Coverage: See if the vehicle is still covered by the manufacturer’s warranty or an extended warranty. Insurance Reference: Helps confirm features and equipment for insurance purposes. Lookup Window Sticker Error 153 Video player configuration error Watch on The window sticker gets you all the necessary information about the vehicle’s specifications and features. On the window sticker, you will find important information like the MSRP, NHTSA safety ratings, and much more. Here is the information you can get through our the window sticker lookup. Check all the features that come with the vehicle, such as safety and security equipment, infotainment systems, mechanical features, and more. Covers extra packages or upgrades that increase the vehicle’s value and customization options. These details help buyers understand how the vehicle differs from its base model. Check the interior color of the vehicle. See the leather and upholstery colors with our window sticker that gets you the exact color match. See the original exterior color of the vehicle. Get the exact color to help you find out if the car has its exterior color painted out. See the vehicle’s detailed initial price. Check the base price, optional features, and the original shipping charges. Examine the vehicle’s safety ratings from the government. Check the rate at which the vehicle endures: frontal crash, side crash, and rollover in a simulation. Also, you can find overall vehicle safety ratings. The shipping charge helps you check the total cost of transporting the vehicle from the manufacturer to the dealership location. Check the total and final price of the vehicle after the shipping charge, MSRP, and additional fees are calculated. See all the information about engine type, transmission (manual or automatic), fuel tank capacity, and drivetrain (FWD, RWD, or AWD) of the vehicle. Get the detailed information on the type of paint, window tint, and exterior lighting, such as the LED brake light. Get the information on the seat materials like fabric or leather, along with air conditioning, dashboard design, and cabin technology. Lists how many airbags are installed on the vehicle, along with the types, seatbelt types, and the sensors, like low tire-pressure and parking sensors, to enhance the safety aspects of the vehicle. Check the warranties that cover the vehicle, like the powertrain warranty, roadside assistance, basic warranty, maintenance warranty, and corrosion warranty. See limited mileage and months. Displays EPA fuel economy ratings with estimated mileage for both city and highway driving. A clear rating makes it easier to compare running costs between vehicles. This rating reflects how much carbon dioxide the car releases per mile, helping buyers compare environmental performance across different models. Each vehicle is given a score from 1 (lowest) to 10 (highest) that shows its fuel efficiency and greenhouse gas impact. To get the window sticker by VIN, all you need to do is follow these simple steps. Find the VIN of the vehicle you want to get the window sticker. For a quick process, check the vehicle paperwork, like the registration paper and insurance. Click on the form above, fill it out with the VIN, and click on the button to submit the form. If you do not have the VIN, you can use your license plate number. Download the window sticker right away to examine the vehicle’s original specifications and features. If you plan to buy a classic car or truck, don’t forget to get the build sheet by VIN to check the original specifications and features of the classic car and truck. The build sheet can help you see the original features and specifications of the vehicle, including the interior and exterior color, to help you see if it’s still on the original build. Get Build Sheet by VIN Many people make simple mistakes when they are reading the window sticker. One common mistake is only focusing on the MSRP instead of other important things like NHTSA safety ratings and the original features the vehicle came with. Here’s a quick overview of what mistakes the readers usually make when reading the window sticker: Ignoring extra charges like options and shipping fees. Missing details that affect long-term costs. Assuming all listed features are included. Not checking what is covered or when it expires. Confirm all listed features, even small items, are present. Rely on the window sticker data, not just sales talk. Use ratings for comparison, not guaranteed mileage. Review NHTSA crash-test scores to compare vehicle safety. Paying close attention to each section of the window sticker helps you see even more detailed information about the vehicle. Shazam K Denver, CO Tate Y San Francisco, CA Mitchell M Cherry Hill, NJ Teresa H Raymore, MO If you have purchased or are thinking about purchasing real estate property, whether it be a home, commercial building or land, you may want to consult with a real estate lawyer in order to protect yourself from any unforeseen liabilities that might be attached to the property. Below are some of the different areas of focus that real estate attorneys can handle. Commercial real estate law is a category of real estate law that establishes standards for commercial leases and the purchase and sale of commercial real estate. Commercial lease lawyers who are familiar with commercial lease terms can help you review potential lease contracts and avoid future pitfalls. Both condominiums and cooperatives are types of "common-interest" communities. In the case of condominiums, owners are usually governed by a CC & R; (Conditions, Convents, & Restrictions) document. Violators can be fined, and defenders against violation accusations may find it valuable to speak to practiced CC & R lawyers. Construction disputes are common in the construction industry, and many of them revolve around construction delays. Because the debate over delays can be tense and time-consuming, construction dispute mediation can be a great, cheap alternative to litigation. Real estate lawyers can also serve where mediation has failed. Landlord and tenant law is at the foundation of many real estate transactions, including eviction, lease signing, subletting, and more. It is difficult to be a successful landlord in any situation, but familiarizing yourself with real estate law gives you firm ground to stand on should a dispute arise. A good grip on the law is important for tenants as well. Renters should review our "What Every Tenant Needs to Know" article to get started. Mortgages are liens that are put on your property by a bank or financial institution, usually in exchange for a large loan. There are several types of mortgages currently available to homeowners, and choosing the right one for your situation is one way of avoiding foreclosure that can occur later on. Speaking to real estate lawyers is the first step to staying on top of your mortgage. The purchase and sale of a residence or home can be done completely on your own, but is more often executed through an experienced broker or agent. While a broker can make the sale of your house much easier, it also brings contracts and commissions into the mix. If you decide to sell through an agent, real estate lawyers can review your contract with them before it is signed. Title and boundary disputes center on issues of property ownership: who owns a property and where are that property's boundaries? Agreed boundary lawyers can assist you if the public record of your property's boundaries differs from the boundaries agreed upon with your neighbor. In fact, if you treat a certain line as a boundary line long enough, it may legally become the property's boundary line under acquiescence to a boundary line laws. Zoning, planning, and land use laws give local governments control over the way that land is used—usually with the goal of creating uniform neighborhoods and areas. Zoning laws can be very complex, and many types of zoning exist to regulate building. The violation of zoning laws can be a serious (and expensive) offense, and those accused of violating zoning laws should speak with local real estate lawyers in their area. When you do have that extension, this means you are covered to drive other vehicles on a 'Third Party Only' basis. This means there is no cover for any actual damage done to the vehicle you are driving - the insurance is just for any 'third parties' damage that you may cause whilst driving that vehicle. It would be wise though, and highly recommended, if you are borrowing a car, to increase that cover level to Comprehensive, which will cover the costs to repair any damage to the borrowed vehicle, should an accident occur. InsureDaily's temporary insurance offers you that level of protection and it avoids the risk of the potential financial loss you would experience if you simply relied on the 'Third Party Only' cover of 'Driving Other Cars' part of a policy. Please also note, another downside is that the 'Driving Other Cars' extension is usually only covering the main policyholder of the main policy, not the named drivers on the policy. You can get a Quote for Temporary Car Insurance in Seconds, including Free Breakdown Cover. Temporary Car Insurance gives you the cover you need, when you need it and for how long you need it. With InsureDaily, our Temporary Insurance policies offer cover for cars and vans that are being borrowed from someone else, and temporary policies for vehicles owned by the person taking out the policy. All of our Temporary Insurance Policies (except our Impound Release policies) offer fully Comprehensive cover. This means that, if you are borrowing someone’s car, should you have an accident that is your fault, the damage will still be covered under the policy. We offer daily car insurance policies and the flexibility to select your preferred period of cover, so you can have one week’s cover, two week’s cover, a month’s cover and up to 84 days. InsureDaily uses a range of short-term insurance products from various Insurance Companies, each with their own number of days cover periods. You will be able to buy a 1 Day policy from as little as £21, based on the current rates. A lot of factors still apply to the calculation of the premium, so this amount will vary depending on the individual cover. For one week’s insurance, prices start from roughly £40, based on the current rates. InsureDaily is part of Ramasis Limited, who are Authorised & Regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority, the FCA. You can view our FCA status on the FCA register where you will find InsureDaily noted as one of our Trading Names. We are also members of BIBA – the British Insurance Brokers Association. BIBA Motor Panel member, Scott Forrester, describes short-term insurance as "a practical solution for the way many drivers now need insurance on an ad-hoc basis, to match their busy lifestyles". No, you do not need to have an annual policy already in place on the car or van being insured under our standard daily insurance policy. Only for our Learner Driver Insurance policies, where the learner is learning in a borrowed car, is there a requirement that the car being used does need to be already insured. Short-term cover is a quick insurance solution for when you want to borrow a car from a friend or family member for a few days.

Terms of use This policy provides emergency assistance when a vehicle suffers a sudden or unexpected mechanical or electrical fault that prevents it from being driven or continuing a journey safely. We do not cover cosmetic, non-emergency or self-induced faults, or where we suspect third-party interference has taken place. Please do not use the policy as a replacement for routine servicing, maintenance or repairs, or to cover faults caused by the actions or omissions of the driver. If you use this policy for a vehicle that's not been serviced or properly maintained, we may not be able to help. We may also have to charge a service fee. This policy only provides assistance to roadworthy and road-legal vehicles. It does not cover unsafe, unroadworthy, unlawful or overladen vehicles, or vehicles being used improperly. Throughout the life of the policy, ensure vehicles are: Serviced annually (or following manufacturers’ guidelines) Maintained in line with manufacturer guidelines In good condition and safe to drive Taxed, insured and have a valid MOT certificate If you use this policy for a vehicle that’s not roadworthy or road-legal, we will not be able to help you get back on the road. We cannot provide assistance if you call us out for a fault we've already attended within the last 28 days – unless you can provide proof that a permanent repair has been completed by a garage. We do not provide cover for any known faults that existed before you bought breakdown cover, so please don't try to claim for them. If you use this policy for a vehicle with a known fault or a temporary repair that hasn't been made permanent, we may not be able to help. We may also have to charge a service fee. The policy covers vehicles up to 3.5 tonnes (3,500kg) in weight and 8 foot 3 inches (2.55 metres) wide. We also don't use cheap 'tab' wire connectors, cheap surround materials, hollow phase plugs (which resonate), or small magnets. We don't build larger full range speakers for more bass, but for astonishing midrange and treble. We supply free DIY speaker cabinet plans, parts list, and setup tips with any purchase. You can also buy generic cabinets online, or have cabinets made by our woodworking partners. Audio Nirvana high efficiency DIY full range speakers have better sound than any other speaker and better build quality than any other speaker. But they are priced 40 to 85% LOWER than any similar design because they're built in our own factory. We have been designing, building, and selling high efficiency DIY full range speakers--using full range drivers--for more than 25 years now. Before we started Audio Nirvana, we were one of the largest sellers of Lowther and Fostex full range speakers. We continue to be dealers for Lowther. With Audio Nirvana we are now one of the largest manufacturers of these types of speakers anywhere in the world. 23 years ago, after many years of using other company's products, we decided to build our own range of high efficiency DIY full range speakers-- Audio Nirvana. There were problems with all the other full range speaker designs available and we decided we could do better. We have. And, for the first time, it's possible for you to buy directly from a manufacturer. In this way, you get the most value for your money AND the best sound possible. A lot of work goes into making a true high efficiency full range speaker, one that approaches 20,000 hz in the treble. And it's expensive. It is necessary to fit powerful magnets to obtain these high frequencies. Our Audio Nirvana full range speakers use magnets as strong--or stronger--than any other full range speaker. Getting the midrange 'right' is especially difficult. It takes extensive testing to optimize the many design elements. In the end, Audio Nirvanas are the most detailed speakers that money can buy. Getting the bass 'right' is also a difficult proposition. What should the cone excursion be? What type of material should be used for the cone, the surround, and the spider? What size of voice coil should be used? What type of wire? We have optimized all of these variables to bring you smooth, full, and very quick and precise bass response. The result is that our Audio Nirvana full range speakers are better sounding than any other full range speakers available. And you can purchase them for far less money..... You'll also be surprised to discover that--despite our very low prices--our Audio Nirvana speakers are also MUCH better than the world's best multi-driver speakers. We've heard most of the world's most highly regarded speakers--Wilson Grand Slamm, JM Labs Grande Utopia, Genesis 1, Dynaudio Evidence, etc. (all over $50,000/pr). None of these speakers sound as natural or musical as our Audio Nirvana full range speaker designs. It sounds impossible, but it's true. You are welcome to bring any speaker by for a direct A-B comparison. We've made these comparisons hundreds of times and that's why we can make this claim. And it's not just our opinion. See what our customers have to say on the 300+ Reviews page. Our goal is not to sell 'affordable' high efficiency DIY full range speakers, but to make the world's best sounding speakers affordable. Despite selling more than 30,000 pairs of Audio Nirvana full range speakers--with 100% money back guarantee--in 23 years, we've had only 15 pairs returned..... Why Are High Efficiency DIY Full Range Speakers So Good? 1. There is no separate woofer, no tweeter, no midrange, and no crossover. 2. Frequency Response: 22 hz to 20,000 hz (varies by model). 3. Clarity, detail, coherence, accuracy, and realism are unmatched because the sound comes from a single voice coil. 4. Imaging and soundstage are incredible because the sound comes from a single point in space. 5. Audio Nirvana high efficiency DIY full range speakers are precision made with very light paper cones and incredibly strong magnets for precise control. 6. Audio Nirvana high efficiency DIY full range speakers are very efficient--at least 97 dB in cabinets. Which Audio Nirvana Full Range  Speaker Should You Choose? We have a policy, at CommonSense Audio, that we won't sell a more expensive speaker, unless it sounds better. If you pay more, you will get better sound. Choose your cabinet first and that will help you decide which of our three full range drivers to choose. For small bookshelf speakers or small towers, the 8 would be the obvious choice. Our Super 12 will maximize the potential of mid size cabinets. And our 15 is simply the best sounding speaker in the world in the larger cabinets. Generally speaking, a well designed larger cabinet is always better than a well designed smaller one. Similarly, a well designed larger full range driver is always better than a well designed smaller one. Why? We can't say for sure, but our guess is that the larger cone can more accurately reproduce vocals and most musical instruments. They are closer in size to the instruments (and human diaphragm) they are trying to reproduce. There is a fullness and richness to larger full range speakers that most smaller ones just can't achieve. In the past, the main reason to use a smaller full range speaker was because a larger one wouldn't go high enough in the treble. However, our 15 inch full range has extension beyond the limits of human hearing . So, unless your cabinet choice requires a smaller speaker, we recommend purchasing our 15. We'll be happy to discuss your particular needs. Just call or email us. Audio Nirvana Driver Descriptions, Specifications, Dimensions CLICK HERE Audio Nirvana DIY Speaker Kits Some of our cabinet designs for the Audio Nirvana full range DIY speakers. Left to Right: 2.8 MkII, 2.8 Series 1, 1.3, Minimonitor, and Micromonitor 99.99% of all speaker cabinets ever made are 'bass reflex' designs. We feel that PROPERLY DESIGNED bass reflex speaker cabinets give the best possible sound. Full range speakers are not woofers. The cone has very limited excursion (movement), known as Xmax. Because of this, full range speakers require different sized tuned ports to achieve the best bass. No other company we know can properly tune bass reflex cabinets for full range speakers. Audio Nirvana full range speakers also work well in bass horn, transmission line, open baffle, and infinite baffle cabinet designs. These require 'short excursion' drivers (speakers) and that's what all Audio Nirvana full range speakers are. We offer more than 15 different bass reflex and open baffle DIY speaker cainet dsigns for Audio Nirvana full range speakers. You will receive a free cabinet plan, parts list, setup tips, and pictures showing proper damping material installation--with any purchase. Please note that Audio Nirvana DIY full range speakers are only half of your project. The DIY speaker cabinet is the other half. If the cabinet is not right, the overall sound will not be right. Because most Audio Nirvanas are built as kits, some may not have been built correctly. It is very important to us that EVERY Audio Nirvana customer get the best possible sound. If you build our cabinets EXACTLY as designed, you will get great sound. If you bought DIY speaker cabinets, for our full range speakers, or purchased Audio Nirvanas already built by someone else, please check with us that the build was done correctly. 'Micromonitor'  (16T x 10W x 7.25D inches or 406 x 254 x 184 mm) 'Minimonitor'  (19T x 10.5W x 11.25D inches or 483 x 267 x 286 mm) 'Monitor' (24T x 13.5W x 11.25D inches or 610 x 343 x 286 mm) 'Monitor MkII' (24T x 11.25W x 13.5D inches or 610 x 286 x 343 mm) '1.3'  (32T x 10W x 10D inches or 813 x 254 x 254 mm) ' 2.8 Mk II' (38.5T x 12W x 13.75D inches or 978 x 305 x 349 mm) '2.8 Series I' (38.5T x 14.5W x 11.25D inches or 978 x 368 x 286 mm) '2.8-15' (32T x 18W x 11D inches or 810 x 460 x 280 mm) '2.8 Mk II Ambience' (38.5T x 12W x 13.75D inches or 978 x 305 x 349 mm) '2.8 Series I Dynamic' (38.5T x 14.5W x 11.25D inches or 978 x 368 x 286 mm) --Also Available in narrow MkII style cabinet '5.6' (44T x 18.75W x 14.75D inches or 1118 x 476 x 375 mm) --Also available in narrow '5.6 MkII' style cabinet (44T x 14.75W x 18.75D or 1118 x 375 x 476 mm) '8.0' (44T x 18.75W x 20.5D inches or 1118 x 476 x 521 mm) '13.6' (48T x 24W x 24D inches or 1219 x 610 x 610 mm) 'Open Baffle' (30T x 24W x 15D inches or 762 x 610 x 381 mm) For those requiring a smaller system, we have four different 'bookshelf' size bass reflex DIY speaker cabinet designs available: The Micromonitor (.41 cubic feet or 12 liters) was designed primarily as a surround DIY speaker for home theater. It still makes a nice 'extension' speaker for small rooms. It is also an excellent computer speaker. You can supplement it with a subwoofer for more bass output. Many people also choose it as a center channel speaker. The Minimonitor (.87 cubic feet or 25 liters) is really our smallest stand alone system. It has full bass and dynamics and a satisfying overall balance. It also makes a wonderful center channel or surround speaker. The Monitor (1.5 cubic feet or 43 liters) is our largest bookshelf system. It gives the bass output of many floor standing systems in a smaller package. As with all the bookshelf systems, they can be placed upright or on their side. This DIY speaker cabinet will accept every speaker size up to, and including, 12 inches. There is also a Monitor MkII version which has a thin front baffle, but is deeper. The difference is mainly in the styling. Both of these can be used on the floor, as the dispersion of all Audio Nirvana high efficiency DIY full range speakers is wide enough that stands are not required. For those who want a floor standing system, we have the following bass reflex systems available: The 1.3 (1.3 cubic feet or 37 liters) is our smallest floor standing system. It makes satisfactory bass for those who have space limitations. The 2.8 tower cabinet (2.8 cubic feet or 80 liters) comes in two versions. The Series I has a wide front baffle, but is shallow in depth. The MkII has a thin front baffle, but is deeper. The difference is mainly in the styling. Both versions are available with two different porting arrangements. The "Big Port" version offers more mid bass at the expense of some low bass output. The "Twin Port" version has 1/3 octave more bass extension, at the expense of some mid bass output. We also offer two different dual driver designs: the 2.8 Dynamic and the 2.8 Ambience. The Dynamic gives a big, powerful sound while maintaining the same overall tonal balance as the normal 2.8. The Ambience puts a lot more 'weight' in the bass and adds a sense of 'air' to the music. It's our favorite version of the 2.8 i f you use 8 inch speakers. The 2.8-15 is specially designed for our 15 inch speakers. It gives all the sensational sound of our 15's in the smallest possible cabinet. The 5.6 or 5.6 MkII (5.6 cubic feet or 160 liters) offer more bass and dynamics, in a DIY speaker cabinet that is not too large for most rooms. The 5.6 MkII is narrower and deeper, but will not accept the 15 inch speaker. The 8.0 (8.0 cubic feet or 227 liters) is for those of you for whom WAF (wife acceptance factor) is important. It looks like the smaller 5.6 from the front, but is 5.75 inches deeper (146 mm). It has more bass and dynamics than the 5.6, but doesn't really look that large. The 13.6 (13.6 cubic feet or 385 liters) is a huge cabinet that maximizes performance of our 15 inch speaker. It can even hold two 15 inch full range speakers. It's the ultimate! And for those of you looking for something 'different', we offer an open baffle speaker type of cabinet: The Open Baffle speaker is basically just a piece of wood supported by two 'wings' to hold it upright. Open baffle speakers don't generate a lot of bass and usually roll off below 63 hz. However, the sound from the back of the open baffle speaker is free to reflect off rear walls and join the sound from the front of the speaker. Open baffle speakers give a 'fat' sound that is very nice for classical music or for people favoring this type of sound. Supplemented with a quality subwoofer, open baffle speakers give an unusual presentation. Open baffle speakers work best with the largest high efficiency full range speakers which can generate more bass without the help of a cabinet. Open baffle speakers are also a way to enjoy our best 15 inch speakers, without requiring a large cabinet. We are experts on open baffle speaker designs and have many years experience. If you go to www.openbaffle.com you will come to this site. Lives covered as on March 31, 2025 T Benefits paid till March 31, 2025 ` Assets under management as on March 31, 2025 ^^ whyIcici.claimwhyIcici.forwhyIcici.sure Claim Settlement Sum Assured Fund value Net Asset Value (NAV) Premium Death benefit Maturity benefit Riders Lock-in period Switching option ULIP returns Top-ups ULIP charges The nominee of the policy is liable to receive a fixed amount in case of the demise of the policy holder during the policy term. This amount is called the Sum Assured. When purchasing a ULIP, you must ensure that the Sum Assured you opt for is sufficient for your dependents to continue with their lives in case something happens to you. The premiums you pay in a ULIP are invested in funds, which grow over time. The fund value indicates the total value of the your fund on the current date. This is calculated by multiplying the number of units you own by the Net Asset Value (NAV) or the monetary value of each unit. In a ULIP, Net Asset Value (NAV) means the value of a single unit of your investment. An investment fund, which is a pool of investments from multiple investors (minus any liabilities), is divided by the number of outstanding units. The NAV of a fund is therefore the price of a single unit. Premiums are the payments you make towards your plan. As an investor, you can pay premiums monthly, quarterly, half-yearly, or annually as per the mode selected by you while investing. Failure to pay the premiums on time may lead to the lapse of the ULIP Policy. The death benefit is the total amount payable to the nominee by the insurance company on the policyholder’s unfortunate demise. It can either be the sum assured or the fund value, whichever is higher. What your beneficiary gets would depend on the plan you opt for. The nominees can obtain the death benefit either as a lump-sum or in monthly instalments. Maturity benefit is offered to the policyholder when the policy tenure gets over. You can avail tax-free* maturity amount as per Section 10 (10D) of the Income Tax Act 1961, subject to the provisions stated therein. Riders or add-ons are the additional benefits provided by insurance companies, which enhance the value of the coverage. Typically, the most common riders offered are critical illness rider, waiver of premium rider, accidental cover, and so forth. However, these riders vary depending upon the company. Lock-in period is a stipulated time till which you cannot withdraw your investments. In case of ULIP, if a policyholder surrenders the policy before the completion of the lock-in period, the fund value is shifted to a discontinuation fund. The policyholder can withdraw the amount once the lock-in period is over. A ULIP has a lock-in period of 5 years. A ULIP plan allows an individual to invest in multiple fund options at the same time. However, the policyholders also have the freedom to switch between these ULIP funds. The number of switches allowed depends on the company’s policies. ICICI Pru’s Protect-N-Gain plan allows unlimited free switches 6 between funds. The returns of a ULIP policy depend upon the performance of the market as well as your fund selection. One needs to stay invested for long term to be able to maximise their returns. As the name suggests, the top-up premium is an additional amount paid over base premium. If a ULIP policyholder wants to increase the amount of his/her investment in funds, he/she can do so through top-ups. Insurance companies levy certain charges on a ULIP. Under a ULIP, a few of the levied charges are as follows: a.Policy administration charge b.Fund management charge c.Mortality charge d.Premium allocation charge ICICI Pru Protect N Gain - Get the dual benefits of Insurance & Investments to secure your family with a life cover & grow your money with market linked returns @ ICICI Pru

LINZESS® (linaclotide) is a prescription medication used to treat irritable bowel syndrome with constipation (IBS-C) in adults and in children and adolescents 7 years of age and older, chronic idiopathic constipation (CIC) in adults, and functional constipation (FC) in children and adolescents 6 years of age and older. “Idiopathic” means the cause of the constipation is unknown. It is not known if LINZESS is safe and effective in children with functional constipation less than 6 years of age or in children with IBS-C less than 7 years of age. IMPORTANT RISK INFORMATION Do not give LINZESS to children who are less than 2 years of age. It may harm them. LINZESS can cause severe diarrhea and your child could get severe dehydration (loss of a large amount of body water and salt). Do not take LINZESS if a doctor has told you that you have a bowel blockage (intestinal obstruction). Before you take LINZESS, tell your doctor about your medical conditions, including if you are: Pregnant or plan to become pregnant. It is not known if LINZESS will harm your unborn baby. Breastfeeding or plan to breastfeed. You and your doctor should decide if you will take LINZESS and breastfeed. Tell your doctor about all the medicines you take, including prescription and over-the-counter medicines, vitamins, and herbal supplements. Side Effects LINZESS can cause serious side effects, including diarrhea, which is the most common side effect and can sometimes be severe. Diarrhea often begins within the first 2 weeks of LINZESS treatment. Stop taking LINZESS and call your doctor right away if you get severe diarrhea during treatment with LINZESS. Other common side effects of LINZESS in people with IBS-C and CIC include gas, stomach-area (abdomen) pain, and swelling, or a feeling of fullness or pressure in your abdomen (distention). Call your doctor or go to the nearest hospital emergency room right away if you develop unusual or severe stomach-area (abdomen) pain, especially if you also have bright red, bloody stools or black stools that look like tar. These are not all the possible side effects of LINZESS. For more information, ask your doctor or pharmacist. You are encouraged to report negative side effects of prescription drugs to the FDA. Visit www.fda.gov/medwatch or call 1-800-FDA-1088. If you are having difficulty paying for your medicine, AbbVie and Ironwood may be able to help. Visit AbbVie.com/PatientAccessSupport to learn more. Supplies Shops has the perfect solution – Digi-Clear laser-safe window envelopes. The main problem with laser printing regular window envelopes isn’t the paper, it’s the film material, which usually melts, shrinks, or wrinkles under the laser’s heat. Well, that’s no longer a problem thanks to our innovative specially treated window film material. The windows in these envelopes are heat-resistant, so they do not sustain damage as they pass under the laser’s glaring heat. When you switch to quality laser safe window envelopes, the windows on your printed envelopes come out looking clear, intact and ready for mailing. Enjoy using Digi-Clear window envelopes in the popular #10 size and a variety of other convenient sizes. In the past, there was no way to safely put standard clear window envelopes through the laser printing process due to the fragility of the window film. Because the plastic film is so thin, it’s vulnerable to heat damage once the laser hits it. This lead to countless wasted envelopes due to warping of the windows. So, we decided to do something about this common problem. Digi-Clear window envelopes can safely be laser printed thanks to the durability of heat-resistant window film material. So, if short-run printing is a top priority, we highly recommend choosing this brand for all your laser printed mailing projects. We originally introduced Digi-Clear in the standard #10 window envelope size and it proved to be a big hit with customers. column13: SUITES Media Things to do More info… Contact BOOK NOW WELCOME TO VILLA SHA CANCUN Located on Cancun’s Northern Coast, directly across from beautiful Isla Mujeres is the spectacular Villa Sha. This luxurious 6-suite property boasts state of the art technology combined with sumptuous Caribbean decor accented by details in each and every space. Designed by renowned Mexican architect Artigas Villa Sha is an absolutely beautiful beach front house that will excite, sooth and refresh its guests with every turn. No detail has been forgotten and the property’s amenities are sure you make your stay one of your most memorable ever. The outdoor terrace, lounge and pool bar may be where you spend most of your time, enjoying alfresco meals and poolside cocktails, all while gazing out over the Caribbean Sea. Villa Sha also boasts direct access to the beach and a private sundeck/yacht dock equipped with sunbeds and overwater hammocks. Inside, you’ll find a fully equipped kitchen, formal dining area, bar and spacious lounge areas, making it easy to gather as a group or break away for more intimate moments. During your stay, there will be two housekeepers, a private chef, a barman and a waiter to assist with any requirements you may have. Additionally, general maintenance staff, a security guard and property manager/concierge services are provided to ensure a comfortable and enjoyable experience. Feeling active? Van Dutch Yachts are on call. Feel free to book time to fish, snorkel, dive or just to venture out to Playa Norte for a day trip. If you would like to stay on land, Golf and spas are only a few minutes away at Playa Mujeres complex. Rafael Nadal’s Tennis Academy is also close by and classes or court rental can also be arranged by our staff. The property location is ideal. Within a 20 minute radius you can find anything from Cancun’s lively hotel zone, full of shops and restaurants, to idyllic, practically undiscovered beaches such as Isla Blanca. We look forward to hosting you and helping you make memories that will last a lifetime. VIEW OUR VIRTUAL TOUR View Details VILLA SHA Feel like a body scrub or relaxing massage? A team of experts will guide you through life-changing programs geared towards achieving ultimate health. We recommend deluxe treatments carried out in the privacy of your own villa by fully trained therapists (extra charges will apply). Every treatment is an opportunity to improve your health and transform your life through the therapeutic power of touch, sound, color, taste and scent. We take an innovative approach to place an emphasis on consciously sourced ingredients and local Mexican, Caribbean, Western, Asian and Japanese produce with Vegan & Vegetarian, and children’s dishes. column13: Home / Services / Foundation Repair / Foundation Problem Signs / Cracks in Foundation Walls Foundation wall cracks are common and can cause serious structural problems if not addressed promptly. Here at Indiana Foundation Service, we are your trusted local experts in foundation repair, with extensive experience in resolving these problems. Cracks in foundation walls are fissures in the concrete walls that make up your home’s foundation. These cracks can range from small hairline fractures to more significant gaps or breaks in the wall itself. They can indicate underlying problems with the foundation of your home and should not be ignored. Repairing cracks in foundation walls can help stabilize the foundation and prevent further cracking or structural damage. It is important to identify and repair these cracks as early as possible to minimize the extent of repairs needed. Foundation wall cracks can be caused by several factors, including: Settlement – Shifting or compacting soil underneath a home’s foundation can cause it to sink and settle unevenly, leading to cracking as the foundation walls bend or move. Expansive Soils – Soils with high clay content can expand and contract as they absorb and lose moisture, putting enormous pressure on foundation walls and causing cracks. Hydrostatic Pressure – Groundwater buildup on the outer side of foundation walls can create pressure that forces the walls inward, leading to cracks and bowing. Cracked foundation walls can be repaired and reinforced to restore the structural integrity of your home. At Indiana Foundation Service, we offer several proven methods depending on the cause and extent of the damage: Foundation Piers – These piers are installed underneath the foundation and anchored to the foundation footing, transferring the weight of the home to competent soils. They stabilize settling foundations and provide support. Wall Anchors – Steel anchors are embedded into the foundation wall and extended into undisturbed soil, countering the inward pressure that causes cracks and bowing. Carbon Fiber Reinforcement – Carbon fiber straps applied to the wall surface strengthen the concrete and prevent cracks from worsening. IntelliBrace™ Wall Repair System – This system installs a network of steel beams inside the wall, providing reinforcement without excavating outdoors. Here’s the foundation repair process: Inspection: Have a foundation repair specialist inspect your home to identify the problem and recommend a solution. Repair: Depending on the type of damage, the specialist will use wall anchors, piers, or other methods to fix the foundation. Waterproofing: After the foundation is repaired, waterproof the walls to prevent moisture intrusion. We offer professional and easy-to-use basement waterproofing solutions. Maintenance: Regular inspections and maintenance can help keep your foundation stable. Check for new cracks and leaks to ensure proper functioning of drainage and downspout extensions. There are important reasons to repair foundation wall cracks promptly: Cracks worsen over time, leading to more extensive repairs. Moisture penetrates cracks, causing mold growth and indoor air quality issues. Doors and windows become difficult to open and close as the foundation settles unevenly. If left unaddressed, severe wall cracks will compromise the structural stability of your home. Don’t wait! Call Indiana Foundation Service today! Our structural repair solutions can return your foundation to strength and prevent further damage. We offer free, no-obligation inspections and estimates on foundation repair services. For nearly 25 years, Indiana Foundation Service has been assisting homeowners in Georgia with repairing foundation damage and preventing future issues. Our certified technicians are experts in evaluating the condition of your foundation and recommending customized repair solutions. We utilize cutting-edge technology and proven techniques to permanently stabilize cracked walls. To schedule a free, no-obligation estimate on foundation wall crack repair for your home, contact us today. Owning your own property means you’re responsible for everything. Plus if you have a mortgage, it might be a condition that you have at least buildings insurance. Most renters only need contents insurance as building cover is the landlord's responsibility. You might also want your own renters insurance policy if you share your home with other tenants. Buildings insurance This covers the structure of the property and its permanent fixtures and fittings, like fitted kitchen units and bathroom suites. It might also provide cover for driveways, patios, walls and fences, and any outbuildings. Contents insurance This covers your household goods, valuables, and personal belongings. Your premium is based on several factors, including the likelihood of claiming, the value of your building and/or contents, your chosen level of cover, the home security measures you have in place, and any relevant risk factors such as flooding or subsidence. For more details, check our average home insurance cost index, which breaks down costs by property type, region, and more. Shopping around for quotes isn’t the only way to save on your home insurance. Here's seven more tips that could help keep the cost of your premium down: Overestimating the value of your contents and the rebuild cost of your home will increase your premium. But if you're in any doubt, it's better to overestimate than underestimate. Get insurer-approved locks for your home, as well as smoke and burglar alarm systems. If you pay for your cover by monthly instalments, an interest charge and finance arrangement fee may be added, so it'll be more expensive. Some insurers will apply a discount to your premium based on the number of years you have held insurance but not made a claim. But be aware, as this isn't the case with all insurers, so check your policy details. Increasing your voluntary excess may reduce your premium but in the event of a claim you will have a larger total excess to pay. Check out our tips for cheaper home insurance Recent years have shown a surge in property insurance prices. The rising cost of materials and labour, combined with more severe and extended weather events, means claim costs are rising for UK insurers. And when costs rise, premiums rise with them. With the cost of insuring your home up by 7.5% since 2023, it's vital to get the best deal you can on your home insurance. Accurately valuing your contents, improving your security, and paying annually could maximise your savings to get the best quotes and cover for your needs."
